Program_Description: Maternal Child Adolescent Health coordinates programs for women of childbearing years and children 0 to 18 years of age, as well as families with Children and Youth with Special Health Care Needs (CYSHCN).
Target_Population: Women of childbearing years and children 0 to 18 years of age.

Program_Description: WIC provides nutritional support and education for low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, as well as infants and children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children.

Program_Description: Provide non-clinical services to pregnant or postpartum women, including education, informational, physical, and emotional supports.
The Home Visiting Program partners with families to support healthy child development, school readiness, and family well-being.
Eligibility_Requirements: Must live within Lassen County and have a child between the ages of 0-5
Application_Process: To apply, please fill out the Interest Form below.

Program_Description: The Women, Infants and Children (WIC) Program is a USDA funded nutrition program to help families have access to healthy foods.
Target_Population: Pregnant women and families with children up to age 5.
Eligibility_Requirements: Eligibility is based on income and household size; must live in California and meet program guidelines.
Application_Process: Contact the office to see if you are eligible to receive these services.
